metadata.source_policy: Public sources and internal workouts are paraphrased/normalized. Endur extraction is pending user-provided screenshots/export and must not copy private content verbatim.

ranking.title: Long Run With Station Breathing Resets

workouts.title: Conversational 5K Builder
workouts.goal: Build aerobic durability for the 8 HYROX running legs without accumulating station fatigue.
warmup: 5 min brisk walk or easy jog
Dynamic calves, hips, hamstrings, and thoracic rotations
3 x 20 sec relaxed strides with walk-back recovery

workout.notes: Hold a pace where full sentences are possible.
Smooth turnover, not a sprint.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: Most athletes underperform HYROX because they run too hard too early.
Zone 2 should leave you better after the session, not flattened.
inspired_by: Public programming: weekly easy/Zone 2 running emphasis

workouts.title: Long Run With Station Breathing Resets
workouts.goal: Extend time on feet while rehearsing post-station breathing control.
warmup: 5 min brisk walk or easy jog
Dynamic calves, hips, hamstrings, and thoracic rotations
3 x 20 sec relaxed strides with walk-back recovery

workout.notes: Every 10 min, walk 30 sec and take 3 complete forced exhales.
Stay tall and quick, no sprinting.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: The breathing reset teaches the nervous system to downshift before the next station.
Use this after a high-stress workday instead of forcing intervals.
inspired_by: Public programming: long aerobic runs and transition heart-rate drops

workouts.title: 1K Repeat Pace Lock
workouts.goal: Improve repeatable 1 km pacing for HYROX running legs.
warmup: 5 min brisk walk or easy jog
Dynamic calves, hips, hamstrings, and thoracic rotations
3 x 20 sec relaxed strides with walk-back recovery

workout.notes: Start 5-10 sec/km slower than target, finish controlled.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: If rep 1 is the fastest, you missed the session objective.
The win is low split variance, not one heroic repeat.
inspired_by: Public programming: 1 km repeat intervals and race-pace running

workouts.title: 800m VO2 Control Set
workouts.goal: Raise speed reserve without turning the session into a sprint workout.
warmup: 5 min brisk walk or easy jog
Dynamic calves, hips, hamstrings, and thoracic rotations
3 x 20 sec relaxed strides with walk-back recovery

workout.notes: 5K effort, relaxed shoulders, even splits.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: Advanced athletes need speed reserve so race pace feels economical.
Stop the set if mechanics collapse.
inspired_by: Public programming: VO2 and advanced interval phases

workouts.title: Steady Tempo Six
workouts.goal: Build sustained aerobic strength between Zone 2 and threshold.
warmup: 5 min brisk walk or easy jog
Dynamic calves, hips, hamstrings, and thoracic rotations
3 x 20 sec relaxed strides with walk-back recovery

workout.notes: Controlled pressure: short phrases are possible, full conversation is not.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: Tempo is where many HYROX runs live after station fatigue.
Do not drift into threshold unless prescribed.
inspired_by: Public programming: tempo run progression

workouts.title: Cruise Threshold Builder
workouts.goal: Build lactate clearance for race-pace running under fatigue.
warmup: 5 min brisk walk or easy jog
Dynamic calves, hips, hamstrings, and thoracic rotations
3 x 20 sec relaxed strides with walk-back recovery

workout.notes: Hard but repeatable; no rep should feel like a race finish.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: Threshold work is the bridge between pure running fitness and HYROX durability.
Hold rhythm before chasing pace.
inspired_by: Public programming: threshold blocks and race-pace emphasis

workouts.title: Run Wall Ball Repeatability
workouts.goal: Practice returning to target pace after quad and breathing fatigue.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: First 200m controlled; settle after breathing normalizes.
Break before misses; breathe on every catch.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: This is a race-skill session, not a wall-ball max test.
The run after wall balls should look like running, not survival.
inspired_by: Public programming: run + station repeats

workouts.title: Run Erg Brick Ladder
workouts.goal: Build tolerance to machine fatigue before running.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Easy enough to protect form.
Legs first, then body, then arms.
Smooth strokes, not max watts.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: Beginners need exposure to the feeling of running after work before they need race loads.
Keep the first month non-threatening.
inspired_by: Public programming: beginner bricks

workouts.title: Sled Drive Lower Strength
workouts.goal: Build squat, hinge, and unilateral strength that transfers to sled push and lunges.
warmup: 5 min easy bike or row
Glute bridges x 12, bodyweight squats x 10, inchworms x 5
2 progressive warmup sets before the first loaded lift

workout.notes: Brace hard, whole foot pressure, break parallel.
Hips back, bar close, flat back.
Front shin stable, torso tall.
Tall posture, quiet shoulders.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: This strength day should make station work smoother, not destroy the next run session.
Leave one rep in reserve on all main lifts.
inspired_by: Internal program: lower strength + carries

workouts.title: Pulling Engine Strength
workouts.goal: Develop lats, upper back, and grip for SkiErg, sled pull, row, and farmer carry.
warmup: 5 min easy bike or row
Glute bridges x 12, bodyweight squats x 10, inchworms x 5
2 progressive warmup sets before the first loaded lift

workout.notes: Pull to lower ribs; no torso swing.
Full reach and strong elbow drive.
Pull to hip; do not rotate.
Use heavy DBs; posture stays tall.
Shoulder health, not max load.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Grip usually fails before fitness on carries and pulls.
Train shoulder health every week.
inspired_by: Internal expert plan: back + Session B logic

workouts.title: Beginner Full Body HYROX Foundation
workouts.goal: Teach squat, hinge, pull, press, carry, and trunk control for new HYROX athletes.
warmup: 5 min easy bike or row
Glute bridges x 12, bodyweight squats x 10, inchworms x 5
2 progressive warmup sets before the first loaded lift

workout.notes: Chest tall, thighs to parallel or below.
Feel hamstrings, never round the back.
Pull to hip.
Full lockout, ribs down.
Short, quick steps.
Lower back stays flat.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Beginners need frequency and confidence more than volume.
This session should be repeatable three times per week if needed.
inspired_by: Internal absolute beginner plan

workouts.title: Strength-Endurance Chipper
workouts.goal: Build muscular endurance across HYROX-relevant movement patterns.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Moderate load, crisp lockout.
Step down if breathing spikes too early.
Use load you can move cleanly.
Use planned sets, never fail reps.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: This style builds local fatigue resistance, but it must not replace race-specific running.
Keep the load moderate and standards clean.
inspired_by: Public programming: strength endurance circuits

workouts.title: Sled Push Pull Overload
workouts.goal: Improve sled mechanics, force production, and recovery between sled efforts.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: Use race load to 110% race load; short powerful steps.
Lean back, rope organized, hand-over-hand rhythm.
Run immediately after each push/pull pair.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Sled gains come from clean force application, not panic pushing.
The easy run teaches you to restore mechanics after heavy leg drive.
inspired_by: Public programming: sled EMOM/overload concepts

workouts.title: Wall Ball Capacity EMOM
workouts.goal: Build repeatable wall-ball sets without failed reps.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: Choose a number you can repeat without no-reps.
Flush quads and restore breathing.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Wall-ball readiness is repeatability, not one huge set.
Break before your squat depth disappears.
inspired_by: Public programming: wall-ball volume accumulation

workouts.title: Carry Grip Ladder
workouts.goal: Develop grip endurance and posture for the 200m carry.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: No drops, tall posture.
Use same load; relax jaw and shoulders.
Stop before posture collapses.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Grip work should build confidence, not tear your hands before pull sessions.
Fast walking with no drops beats reckless jogging.
inspired_by: Public programming: grip and carry emphasis

workouts.title: Burpee Broad Jump Rhythm Builder
workouts.goal: Build sustainable burpee broad jump rhythm and soft landings.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: Stand fully, jump only as far as you can repeat, land soft.
Start the run controlled after each set.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Small repeatable jumps beat early hero jumps.
The first 50m after burpees is where form discipline matters.
inspired_by: Public programming: burpee broad jump bricks

workouts.title: Sandbag Lunge Progression
workouts.goal: Prepare legs and trunk for the 100m sandbag lunge station.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: Torso upright, rear knee controlled, breathe every rep.
No surging after lunges.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Lunges punish rushed breathing and sloppy trunk position.
Race-day success is stable reps, not fast no-reps.
inspired_by: Public programming: lunge progression and station density

workouts.title: SkiErg Technique Density
workouts.goal: Improve efficient SkiErg strokes under mild fatigue.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: Long reach, lats first, hinge as handles pass face.
Brace without breath holding.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Technique beats brute force on the SkiErg.
Watch stroke power fade; switch or rest before the movement becomes arms-only.
inspired_by: Public programming: SkiErg technique and calories

workouts.title: Row Stroke Economy
workouts.goal: Build row efficiency so the station does not damage the next run.
warmup: 5 min easy machine flush
Station-specific technique reps at 30-40% effort
Nasal breathing for the final 2 min before the main set

workout.notes: Legs-body-arms; recover arms-body-legs. Keep strokes long.
Stand tall before running; no panic first 50m.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: The row is not won in the first 250m.
A controlled exit matters as much as the split.
inspired_by: Public programming: row/run alternation

workouts.title: Eight-Station Skill Flow
workouts.goal: Practice all HYROX movement patterns at low pressure.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Technique only.
Light load, clean body angle.
Rope organized.
Soft landings.
Long strokes.
No drops.
Upright torso.
Break before misses.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: This is the safest way to teach the full race menu.
Technique exposure reduces race-day anxiety.
inspired_by: Public programming: station technique phases

workouts.title: Four-Station Compromised Primer
workouts.goal: Introduce race order without full simulation cost.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Control transitions.
Light to moderate load.
Small repeatable jumps.
Record total time.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: This is a confidence builder.
Record transition time, not just station time.
inspired_by: Public programming: half simulation and first four stations

workouts.title: 50 Percent HYROX Format
workouts.goal: Run the full race order at half station volume to learn pacing and transitions.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.distance: 8 x 1km runs + all 8 stations at 50% volume
workout.notes: Use race order; record every split.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Half-volume simulations reveal pacing mistakes without crushing recovery.
If transitions are messy here, they will be worse on race day.
inspired_by: Public programming: partial simulations

workouts.title: Controlled Full HYROX Simulation
workouts.goal: Complete all 8 stations and 8 running legs under controlled race rules.
warmup: 10 min easy jog
Dynamic prep: hips, ankles, thoracic spine, shoulders
One 30% effort practice set for each station
Race shoes, fuel, hydration, and transitions exactly as planned

workout.notes: Target 85-90% race effort unless this is the final benchmark.

cooldown: 10 min continuous walk
Protein + carbohydrates within 45 min
Legs elevated 20 min
Record splits, transition delays, fueling notes, and weakest station

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Do not prove fitness every weekend; full simulations are expensive.
One high-quality full simulation beats several sloppy maximal rehearsals.
inspired_by: Public programming: full simulation timing and taper placement

workouts.title: Transition Discipline Rehearsal
workouts.goal: Reduce wasted time and heart-rate spikes between stations.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Last 30m includes nasal breathing and visualizing station split.
Rotate ski, row, wall ball, carry.
First 50m controlled, then settle to target rhythm.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Reduce run distance 20-40%
Use conversational run-walk intervals
Keep RPE below 6 until mechanics are stable
intermediate: Cap pace fade at 5-8% across repeats
advanced: Hold race pace or slightly faster

coach_notes: Free speed comes from knowing what happens before you arrive.
Never let both doubles athletes stand still at the same time.
inspired_by: Public programming: transition practice and heart-rate drops

workouts.title: Race Week Station Primer
workouts.goal: Stay sharp without creating soreness in race week.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Finish fresher than you started.

workout.reps: 10 cal ski + 10 wall balls + 10m sled push optional
workout.notes: Everything smooth, no strain.
Light and elastic.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: The taper goal is confidence, not fitness gain.
If you feel tempted to add work, go home.
inspired_by: Public programming: race-week taper and light station primers

workouts.title: Ski Pull Row Negative Split
workouts.goal: Teach controlled pacing across pulling stations before run re-entry.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Each round slightly faster.
Smooth rope rhythm.
Negative split each row.
No panic off the rower.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: Useful for athletes who overcook SkiErg and lose the row/run.
Pacing discipline matters more than peak watts.
inspired_by: Gap analysis: pulling-station cluster

workouts.title: Doubles Handoff Speed Lab
workouts.goal: Practice doubles communication, planned workload splits, and no-dead-time station changes.
warmup: 6-8 min easy run, bike, ski, or row
Dynamic hips, ankles, thoracic rotations, leg swings, and arm circles
2 light practice rounds of the first station movement
3 full forced exhales before starting the first run

workout.notes: Switch before power drops, not after.
One athlete works while the other resets breathing.
Use pre-agreed sets like 15/10/10/5.

cooldown: 5-8 min easy walk or machine flush
Hip flexor, calf, quad, hamstring, and lat stretch
5 deep belly breaths: 4 sec inhale, 2 sec hold, 6 sec exhale

beginner: Use 50-70% race load
Break reps early before form fails
Extend rest to preserve movement quality

coach_notes: The best doubles teams do not negotiate during stations.
Every handoff should be under 5 sec.
inspired_by: Internal doubles strategy
